concerns.text: User Story 3 (Reproducibility Package Export) does not reference any functional requirement (e.g., FR‑006) or success criterion (e.g., SC‑004, SC‑009); the story lacks traceability to concrete requirements.
User Story 4 (Efficient CI Execution) lacks explicit anchors to FR‑009 or any success criteria (SC‑008, SC‑005); no requirement or criterion is cited.
Functional Requirement FR‑001 (accept list of URLs) is not linked to any user story; no story cites FR‑001, leaving it orphaned.
Functional Requirement FR‑002 (automatic extraction) is orphaned – no user story references it.
Functional Requirement FR‑003 (reconstruct p‑value) is orphaned – no user story cites it.
Functional Requirement FR‑006 (export reproducible research package) is not anchored to any user story; US‑3 describes reproducibility but does not reference FR‑006.
Functional Requirement FR‑009 (CPU‑only execution constraints) is orphaned – no user story explicitly references it.
Success Criterion SC‑002 (inconsistency‑detection precision ≥ 90 %) is not tied to any user story; it only mentions FR‑004.
Success Criterion SC‑003 (statistical tests must be computed with SciPy) lacks a story anchor; no user story references SC‑003.
FR‑004 criterion 1 refers to a confidence‑interval level as “[deferred]”; without a concrete confidence level (e.g., 95 %), the rule is ambiguous and cannot be operationalised.
FR‑005 mentions computing a Wilson “[deferred]” confidence interval but omits the confidence level (e.g., 95 %); the CI width is undefined.
FR‑005 adds analyses (chi‑squared goodness‑of‑fit, Cochran‑Armitage trend test, Bonferroni‑adjusted subgroup tests) that are not present in the idea or user stories, representing scope over‑reach.
